title: Jaron Kyte
content:
{{Top|ga|sprot|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Jaron Kyte |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Specter of the Past]]'' |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Imperial Remnant]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|General Kyte was the only one who reacted to [Lord Graemon's] entrance. As such, he's my prime suspect. With luck, he may panic and lead my Intelligence team to some of the others involved.''"
"''Kyte won't panic. But he might think it wise to alert them.''|Admiral Gilad Pellaeon and High General Sutt Ramic|}} [[General/Legends|General]] '''Jaron Kyte''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served the [[Imperial Remnant]] as a senior [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet's]] defense hierarchy of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], the financial center of the Remnant. Kyte was involved in a private agreement between the [[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], [[Vilim Disra]], and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zothip]] of the [[Cavrilhu Pirates]] in which the Remnant provided the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] with [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] and tactical support in exchange for performing [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] for the Remnant and providing them with [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class starfighters]] from a [[Cavrilhu Pirates Preybird production facility|production facility]]. In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], discovered the agreement and considered it [[Treason/Legends|treasonous]] since it had not been conducted through official channels. He set out to investigate the conspiracy and soon discovered Kyte's involvement in supplying Imperial equipment and personnel to pirate gangs. ==Biography== ===A secret agreement=== {{Quote|But we have a pretty solid connection between General Kyte and someone who is definitely linked to the Cavrilhu Pirates. Kyte sent a transmission to the contact right after your meeting with the Muunilinst defense hierarchy eleven days ago. We're following it up.|Commander Dreyf reports to Admiral Gilad Pellaeon|}} [[File:Muunilinst.jpg|right|thumb|Muunilinst, the planet on which Jaron Kyte was stationed]] Jaron Kyte was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]]. By [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. he had attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[General/Legends|general]] and was stationed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] in the [[Obtrexta sector/Legends|Obtrexta sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Kyte was one of the senior [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] overseeing the defenses of Muunilinst, which served as the financial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]] under [[High General]] [[Sutt Ramic]]. From his position, Kyte was one of several high-ranking [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] officials involved in a secret agreement between [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Vilim Disra]] of the [[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zothip]] of the [[Cavrilhu Pirates]]. As part of the agreement, the Cavrilhu [[Pirate/Legends|Pirates]] supplied the Imperials with [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class starfighters]] from a [[Cavrilhu Pirates Preybird production facility|production plant]] that they had acquired and reactivated. In exchange, the pirates received tactical assistance in the form of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] drawn from [[sleeper cell]]s that [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] had established during his [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[9 ABY/Legends|ten years]] earlier. In addition, the pirates were hired to strike at New Republic shipping as [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]], using information that Disra had obtained from [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] reports. However, the agreement had not been processed through official channels, and the raiding targets were not chosen by [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command]] or Imperial Intelligence. Furthermore, the entire arrangement was kept secret from [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the Imperial Fleet. Kyte was responsible for supplying Imperial equipment and personnel to the Cavrilhu Pirates, and his role in the agreement brought him into contact with [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Graemon]], a [[Banker/Legends|banker]] on Muunilinst who funneled the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|funds]] from the Empire to the pirates. Kyte was also in contact with another [[Jaron Kyte's contact|individual]] who had a direct connection to the pirates, and any messages that needed to be relayed to the Pirates went through him. ===Pellaeon's investigation=== {{Quote|The treason is in the fact that the deal has been made outside proper channels. And in the fact that certain high Imperial officials are siphoning off a signifcant percentage of those funds for their own personal gain. And in the fact that the deal includes the supplying of Imperial equipment and personnel to various pirate gangs.|Pellaeon sublty implicates Kyte in treason|}} In 19 ABY, Kyte was summoned to a meeting with Admiral Pellaeon alongside High General Ramic and the other four senior [[Officer/Legends|officers]] of the Muunilinst defense hierarchy. Pellaeon had proposed [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|making peace]] with the New Republic and had convinced the Moffs to agree to his plan. As part of the process, the Admiral was touring the Remnant's [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] installations and presenting the proposal to the senior officers, seeking their support and explaining his reasoning behind the need for a peace treaty. [[File:GiladPellaeonHS-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|250px|Gilad Pellaeon (pictured) investigated Kyte's actions on Muunilinst.]] However, while attending to other business on Muunilinst, Pellaeon learned of the arrangement between Disra and the Cavrilhu Pirates—although he did not know the identity of the pirate gang—and investigations by Intelligence officers under his command had uncovered financial records that had led him to Lord Graemon. Pellaeon suspected that a senior member of the Muunilinst defense setup was working with Graemon, so he invited the banker to his meeting with the senior officials. When Graemon arrived during the meeting, Kyte was shocked to see the banker and briefly allowed it to show on his face before recovering his composure. Pellaeon, watching the assembled officers for any signs of recognition toward Graemon from any of them, caught the involuntary movement and deduced that Kyte was in league with Graemon since he was the only officer in the room to react to the banker's presence. After Graemon was shown to a side room, Ramic inquired as to the banker's presence, and Pellaeon explained the agreement he had discovered to the assembled officers. He noted that some high-ranking officials were embezzling [[Currency/Legends|funds]] for their own personal gain, and added—while looking at Kyte—that others were guilty of supplying Imperial equipment and personnel to pirate gangs, acts that Pellaeon considered to be [[Treason/Legends|treasonous]]. Kyte realized that Pellaeon suspected he was involved in the conspiracy, and, after the meeting was concluded, the General sent a message to his contact with the Cavrilhu Pirates, warning them that Pellaeon may have discovered the arrangement. The transmission was subsequently intercepted by Intelligence agents led by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Dreyf]], who reported the activity to Pellaeon eleven [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I thought you were the one who made the proposal. How can you say now that you oppose it?|Kyte, to Pellaeon|}} Jaron Kyte did not usually panic when faced with difficult situations. Despite being loyal to the Remnant, Kyte had no compunctions about operating outside official channels when the need arose, and he allied himself with Moff Disra rather than supporting the Supreme Commander of the Fleet, Pellaeon. Kyte was somewhat suspicious of Pellaeon, and questioned why Pellaeon put forward a treaty with the New Republic if he disliked the idea. ==Skills and abilities== Kyte was a capable officer who reached the rank of general and was assigned as a senior officer in the defense hierarchy of an important Imperial planet. Although Kyte was usually able maintain his composure and control his body [[Language/Legends|language]] to a degree hide his reactions, Kyte was unable to suppress his initial shock when caught unaware by the arrival of Lord Graemon, allowing Admiral Pellaeon—who was looking for a reaction from Kyte—to see it clearly before Kyte regained composure. ==Behind the scenes== Jaron Kyte appeared in ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', the first novel of ''[[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology]]'', written by [[Timothy Zahn]] and published in [[1997]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Specter of the Past]]'' {{Ab|Specter of the Past (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jaron Kyte }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kyte, Jaron}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial admirals and generals]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]]
